prompt:
Billing QA bounced the June dry run of our meter-usage ETL (this directory) and I spent yesterday cross-checking raw exports by hand, so I can describe exactly what comes out wrong — just not why.

First, any meter that needed archive backfill is absurd. hx-201's June 3rd line claims a few thousand kWh for a two-bedroom flat; the raw archive line for that hour says 5.4312 kilowatt-hours. Only backfilled rows blow up like this — live gateway rows on the very same report are sane, and archive rows that were stored in watt-hours are sane too.

Second, the hourly series looks shifted for meters that report exactly on the hour, which is most of the commercial fleet (they push at :00 sharp). Usage stamped 10:00:00 shows up under the 09:00 hour, and when the on-the-hour report is the only reading in its hour, that hour is simply missing from the series. Meters that report at :05 or :30 are fine, which is why residential never complained. Related, I think: a couple of midnight-sharp readings got billed to the previous day.

Third, even on clean live-only data the report disagrees with finance's spreadsheet by a hundredth of a kWh here and there — maybe one line in ten. Hand check from yesterday: a day totalling 1.465 kWh prints as 1.46; finance (who round halves up, per policy) say 1.47.

python3 test_meter_etl.py reproduces every one of these. Please root-cause and fix the pipeline. The tests are the contract — leave them alone — and don't paper over anything by fudging expected numbers; the sane cases in there have to keep working exactly as they do.
